trans-unit.source: Estimating Exposed Population – Aina’s Manual Approach
trans-unit.target: Estimating Exposed Population – Aina’s Manual Approach

trans-unit.source: Characteristics of the exercise
trans-unit.target: Characteristics of the exercise

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Type of trainings exercise:__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: :::{grid-item-card} __Exercise Track:__
trans-unit.target: :::{grid-item-card} __Exercise Track:__

trans-unit.source: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html)
trans-unit.target: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html)

trans-unit.source: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^
trans-unit.target: ::::{grid} 2 :::{grid-item-card} __Estimated time demand for the exercise__ ^^^

trans-unit.source: [Zonal Statistics](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Zonal Statistics](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_raster_basic_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: [Intersection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)
trans-unit.target: [Intersection](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_spatial_joins_wiki.html#join-attributes-by-location-summary)

trans-unit.source: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Projections](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Buffer](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Buffer](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Clip](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)
trans-unit.target: [Clip](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projections_wiki.md)

trans-unit.source: [Automatisation](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_automatisation_wiki.html)
trans-unit.target: [Automatisation](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_automatisation_wiki.html)

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall. ^^^
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall. ^^^

trans-unit.source: Instructions for the trainers
trans-unit.target: Instructions for the trainers

trans-unit.source: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__
trans-unit.target: :::{dropdown} __Trainers Corner__

trans-unit.source: Prepare the training
trans-unit.target: Prepare the training

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on facilitating trainings.

trans-unit.source: Conduct the training
trans-unit.target: Conduct the training

trans-unit.source: __Introduction:__
trans-unit.target: __Introduction:__

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: __Follow-along:__
trans-unit.target: __Follow-along:__

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: __Wrap up:__
trans-unit.target: __Wrap up:__

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: Available Data
trans-unit.target: Available Data

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets here, save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets here, save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__

trans-unit.source: The folder contains the whole [standard folder structure](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder and the additional documentation in the documentation folder.
trans-unit.target: The folder contains the whole [standard folder structure](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#standard-folder-structure) with all data in the input folder and the additional documentation in the documentation folder.

trans-unit.source: Dataset
trans-unit.target: Dataset

trans-unit.source: Source
trans-unit.target: Source

trans-unit.source: Descriptions
trans-unit.target: Descriptions

trans-unit.source: Administrative Boundaries
trans-unit.target: Administrative Boundaries

trans-unit.source: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-mdg)
trans-unit.target: [HDX](https://data.humdata.org/dataset/cod-ab-mdg)

trans-unit.source: The administrative boundaries on level 0-4 for Madagascar can be accessed via HDX provided by OCHA. For this trigger mechanism we provide the administrative boundaries on level 1 (regional level) and 2 (district level) as a shapefile.
trans-unit.target: The administrative boundaries on level 0-4 for Madagascar can be accessed via HDX provided by OCHA. For this trigger mechanism we provide the administrative boundaries on level 1 (regional level) and 2 (district level) as a shapefile.

trans-unit.source: Cyclone Tracks
trans-unit.target: Cyclone Tracks

trans-unit.source: [International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship (IBTrACS)](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-best-track-archive)
trans-unit.target: [International Best Track Archive for Climate Stewardship (IBTrACS)](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/products/international-best-track-archive)

trans-unit.source: IBTrACS project is the most complete global collection of tropical cyclones available. It merges recent and historical tropical cyclone data from multiple agencies to create a unified, publicly available, best-track dataset that improves inter-agency comparisons.
trans-unit.target: IBTrACS project is the most complete global collection of tropical cyclones available. It merges recent and historical tropical cyclone data from multiple agencies to create a unified, publicly available, best-track dataset that improves inter-agency comparisons.

trans-unit.source: education facilities and health sites
trans-unit.target: education facilities and health sites

trans-unit.source: [HOT Export Tool](https://export.hotosm.org/vi/v3/exports/new/describe)
trans-unit.target: [HOT Export Tool](https://export.hotosm.org/vi/v3/exports/new/describe)

trans-unit.source: The POI data (education facilities and health sites) is downloaded using the HOT Export Tool based on OpenStreetMap data.
trans-unit.target: The POI data (education facilities and health sites) is downloaded using the HOT Export Tool based on OpenStreetMap data.

trans-unit.source: Population
trans-unit.target: Population

trans-unit.source: [WorldPop](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=49646)
trans-unit.target: [WorldPop](https://hub.worldpop.org/geodata/summary?id=49646)

trans-unit.source: The worldpop dataset in raster format provides the estimated total number of people per grid-cell for the year 2020. We will be working with the Constrained Individual countries 2020 dataset at a resolution of 100m.
trans-unit.target: The worldpop dataset in raster format provides the estimated total number of people per grid-cell for the year 2020. We will be working with the Constrained Individual countries 2020 dataset at a resolution of 100m.

trans-unit.source: **Aina** is the GIS expert at the **Croix-Rouge Malagasy (CRM)**. With the cyclone season approaching, she knows that time is of the essence once a storm is forecasted. Every hour counts when it comes to protecting communities at risk.
trans-unit.target: **Aina** is the GIS expert at the **Croix-Rouge Malagasy (CRM)**. With the cyclone season approaching, she knows that time is of the essence once a storm is forecasted. Every hour counts when it comes to protecting communities at risk.

trans-unit.source: This year, Aina wants to be one step ahead. Instead of manually analyzing cyclone data under pressure, she decides to **prepare an automated QGIS model** that will help her respond quickly and efficiently.
trans-unit.target: This year, Aina wants to be one step ahead. Instead of manually analyzing cyclone data under pressure, she decides to **prepare an automated QGIS model** that will help her respond quickly and efficiently.

trans-unit.source: **Her goal:**
trans-unit.target: **Her goal:**

trans-unit.source: Build a workflow that automatically estimates exposed populations and infrastructure at risk.
trans-unit.target: Build a workflow that automatically estimates exposed populations and infrastructure at risk.

trans-unit.source: Tasks:
trans-unit.target: Tasks:

trans-unit.source: Before developing the automated model, Aina used to estimate the exposed population manually whenever a cyclone approached Madagascar. In this task, you will follow the steps she used in the past by working with the historical track of **Cyclone Harald**, WorldPop raster data, and administrative boundaries.
trans-unit.target: Before developing the automated model, Aina used to estimate the exposed population manually whenever a cyclone approached Madagascar. In this task, you will follow the steps she used in the past by working with the historical track of **Cyclone Harald**, WorldPop raster data, and administrative boundaries.

trans-unit.source: You will manually buffer the cyclone track, clip the population raster, and calculate exposed population using zonal statistics.
trans-unit.target: You will manually buffer the cyclone track, clip the population raster, and calculate exposed population using zonal statistics.

trans-unit.source: **Open QGIS** and [create a new project](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`
trans-unit.target: **Open QGIS** and [create a new project](/content/Wiki/en_qgis_projects_folder_structure_wiki.md#step-by-step-setting-up-a-new-qgis-project-from-scratch) by clicking on `Project` → `New`

trans-unit.source: **Save the project** in the “project” folder. To do that click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project “Cyclon_Harald_Exposure”.
trans-unit.target: **Save the project** in the “project” folder. To do that click on `Project` → `Save as` and navigate to the folder. Name the project “Cyclon_Harald_Exposure”.

trans-unit.source: **Load the GeoJOSN** file "example_Harald_2025_Track.geojson" in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)) . Open the folder `data` → `input`.
trans-unit.target: **Load the GeoJOSN** file "example_Harald_2025_Track.geojson" in your project by drag and drop ([Wiki Video](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Wiki/en_qgis_import_geodata_wiki.html#open-vector-data-via-drag-and-drop)) . Open the folder `data` → `input`.

trans-unit.source: **Reproject the cyclone track** to use meters instead of degrees (important for accurate buffering):
trans-unit.target: **Reproject the cyclone track** to use meters instead of degrees (important for accurate buffering):

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Reproject Layer`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Reproject Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Input: `example_Harald_2025_Track`
trans-unit.target: Input: `example_Harald_2025_Track`

trans-unit.source: Target CRS: `EPSG:29738` or another meter-based CRS appropriate for Madagascar.
trans-unit.target: Target CRS: `EPSG:29738` or another meter-based CRS appropriate for Madagascar.

trans-unit.source: Save the result in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Track_Reprojected`** :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_reproject_cyclon_track.PNG
trans-unit.target: Save the result in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Track_Reprojected`** :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_reproject_cyclon_track.PNG

trans-unit.source: Reprojecting the cyclone track. :::
trans-unit.target: Reprojecting the cyclone track. :::

trans-unit.source: :::{Attention} Buffer distances must be calculated in meters. Many datasets (like GeoJSON cyclone tracks) use geographic coordinate systems like EPSG:4326, which measure in degrees — not meters. To correctly calculate a 200 km buffer, we must first reproject the track into a projected CRS that uses meters. ::: 5. **Buffer the cyclone track**:
trans-unit.target: :::{Attention} Buffer distances must be calculated in meters. Many datasets (like GeoJSON cyclone tracks) use geographic coordinate systems like EPSG:4326, which measure in degrees — not meters. To correctly calculate a 200 km buffer, we must first reproject the track into a projected CRS that uses meters. ::: 5. **Buffer the cyclone track**:

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Buffer`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Buffer`.

trans-unit.source: Input: `Harald_Track_Reprojected`
trans-unit.target: Input: `Harald_Track_Reprojected`

trans-unit.source: Buffer distance: `200000` (meters)
trans-unit.target: Buffer distance: `200000` (meters)

trans-unit.source: Segments: Leave default (5)
trans-unit.target: Segments: Leave default (5)

trans-unit.source: Dissolve: `Yes`
trans-unit.target: Dissolve: `Yes`

trans-unit.source: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Buffer_200km`** :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_cyclon_track_buffer.PNG
trans-unit.target: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Buffer_200km`** :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_cyclon_track_buffer.PNG

trans-unit.source: Buffering the cyclone track. :::
trans-unit.target: Buffering the cyclone track. :::

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Intermediate Result: Buffer :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_cyclon_track_buffer.PNG
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Intermediate Result: Buffer :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_cyclon_track_buffer.PNG

trans-unit.source: The intermediate results must show the cyclone’s trajectory and the 200-kilometer buffer zone around it. The buffer zone must be a single entity. ::: :::: 6. **Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326 (to match the raster's CRS):**
trans-unit.target: The intermediate results must show the cyclone’s trajectory and the 200-kilometer buffer zone around it. The buffer zone must be a single entity. ::: :::: 6. **Reproject the buffer back to EPSG:4326 (to match the raster's CRS):**

trans-unit.source: In the Processing Toolbox, search for Reproject Layer.
trans-unit.target: In the Processing Toolbox, search for Reproject Layer.

trans-unit.source: Input: Harald_Buffer_200km_29738
trans-unit.target: Input: Harald_Buffer_200km_29738

trans-unit.source: Target CRS: EPSG:4326 – WGS 84
trans-unit.target: Target CRS: EPSG:4326 – WGS 84

trans-unit.source: Save the output in the temp folder as: Harald_Buffer_200km_4326 :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_reproject_cyclon_buffer.PNG
trans-unit.target: Save the output in the temp folder as: Harald_Buffer_200km_4326 :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_reproject_cyclon_buffer.PNG

trans-unit.source: Reprojecting the cyclone buffer :::
trans-unit.target: Reprojecting the cyclone buffer :::

trans-unit.source: **Load the administrative boundaries**:
trans-unit.target: **Load the administrative boundaries**:

trans-unit.source: File: `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_20181031.gpkg`
trans-unit.target: File: `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_20181031.gpkg`

trans-unit.source: Add using drag and drop or `Add Vector Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Add using drag and drop or `Add Vector Layer`.

trans-unit.source: **Load the population raster**:
trans-unit.target: **Load the population raster**:

trans-unit.source: File: `MDG_WorldPop_2020_constrained.tif`
trans-unit.target: File: `MDG_WorldPop_2020_constrained.tif`

trans-unit.source: Add using `Layer` → `Add Raster Layer`.
trans-unit.target: Add using `Layer` → `Add Raster Layer`.

trans-unit.source: **Clip the population raster** using the buffered impact zone:
trans-unit.target: **Clip the population raster** using the buffered impact zone:

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Clip Raster by Mask Layer`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Clip Raster by Mask Layer`.

trans-unit.source: Input raster: `MDG_WorldPop_2020_constrained`
trans-unit.target: Input raster: `MDG_WorldPop_2020_constrained`

trans-unit.source: Mask layer: `Harald_Buffer_200km`
trans-unit.target: Mask layer: `Harald_Buffer_200km`

trans-unit.source: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Pop_Clip`** :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_clip_pop_raster.PNG
trans-unit.target: Save output in the `temp` folder as: **`Harald_Pop_Clip`** :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_clip_pop_raster.PNG

trans-unit.source: Clipping the population raster with the cyclone buffer ::: ::::{dropdown} Intermediate Result: Clip Population Raster Alyer :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_clip_pop_raster.PNG
trans-unit.target: Clipping the population raster with the cyclone buffer ::: ::::{dropdown} Intermediate Result: Clip Population Raster Alyer :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_intermediate_result_clip_pop_raster.PNG

trans-unit.source: Your intermediate results should look similar to this. ::: ::::
trans-unit.target: Your intermediate results should look similar to this. ::: ::::

trans-unit.source: **Calculate total exposed population**:
trans-unit.target: **Calculate total exposed population**:

trans-unit.source: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Zonal Statistics`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Processing Toolbox**, search for `Zonal Statistics`.

trans-unit.source: Input vector layer: `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_20181031.gpkg`
trans-unit.target: Input vector layer: `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_20181031.gpkg`

trans-unit.source: Raster layer: `Harald_Pop_Clip`
trans-unit.target: Raster layer: `Harald_Pop_Clip`

trans-unit.source: Statistic to calculate: `Sum`
trans-unit.target: Statistic to calculate: `Sum`

trans-unit.source: Field prefix: e.g., `exposed_population_`
trans-unit.target: Field prefix: e.g., `exposed_population_`

trans-unit.source: Save the updated vector layer in the `result` folder as: **`Harald_Exposed_Populationg`**
trans-unit.target: Save the updated vector layer in the `result` folder as: **`Harald_Exposed_Populationg`**

trans-unit.source: The result will be a new column in the attribute table of the `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_201810312.gpkg` layer, showing the total population within the cyclone buffer per district. :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_pop_zonal_statistic.PNG
trans-unit.target: The result will be a new column in the attribute table of the `mdg_admbnda_adm2_BNGRC_OCHA_201810312.gpkg` layer, showing the total population within the cyclone buffer per district. :::{figure} /fig/fr_MDG_AA_pop_zonal_statistic.PNG

trans-unit.source: Calculating the population exposed to the cyclone per district using the worldpop raster. :::
trans-unit.target: Calculating the population exposed to the cyclone per district using the worldpop raster. :::

trans-unit.source: **Visualise the affected population by classifying the results**: Now that Aina has estimated the exposed population in each district, she wants to clearly show the differences across regions on the map. To do this, we'll apply a **graduated classification** to the `Harald_Exposed_Population` layer using the new population field created by the Zonal Statistics tool.
trans-unit.target: **Visualise the affected population by classifying the results**: Now that Aina has estimated the exposed population in each district, she wants to clearly show the differences across regions on the map. To do this, we'll apply a **graduated classification** to the `Harald_Exposed_Population` layer using the new population field created by the Zonal Statistics tool.

trans-unit.source: In the **Layers panel**, right-click on the layer `Harald_Exposed_Population` and choose `Properties`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Layers panel**, right-click on the layer `Harald_Exposed_Population` and choose `Properties`.

trans-unit.source: Go to the **Symbology** tab on the left.
trans-unit.target: Go to the **Symbology** tab on the left.

trans-unit.source: At the top of the window, change the style from `Single Symbol` to `Graduated`.
trans-unit.target: At the top of the window, change the style from `Single Symbol` to `Graduated`.

trans-unit.source: In the **Value** drop-down menu, select the field that contains the population sum. It typically starts with the prefix you defined earlier, e.g. `exposed_population_sum`.
trans-unit.target: In the **Value** drop-down menu, select the field that contains the population sum. It typically starts with the prefix you defined earlier, e.g. `exposed_population_sum`.

trans-unit.source: Set the **color ramp** to one that suits your map (e.g. `Reds`).
trans-unit.target: Set the **color ramp** to one that suits your map (e.g. `Reds`).

trans-unit.source: Choose a **classification mode** (e.g. `Quantile`, `Natural Breaks`, or `Equal Interval`) and select the number of classes (e.g. 5).
trans-unit.target: Choose a **classification mode** (e.g. `Quantile`, `Natural Breaks`, or `Equal Interval`) and select the number of classes (e.g. 5).

trans-unit.source: Click `Classify` to generate the classification.
trans-unit.target: Click `Classify` to generate the classification.

trans-unit.source: Click `Apply` and then `OK` to display the classified map.
trans-unit.target: Click `Apply` and then `OK` to display the classified map.

trans-unit.source: Classifying the exposed population into five classes. :::
trans-unit.target: Classifying the exposed population into five classes. :::

trans-unit.source: Results
trans-unit.target: Results

trans-unit.source: Your results should look something like this:
trans-unit.target: Your results should look something like this:

trans-unit.source: Visualising the 5 classes. :::
trans-unit.target: Visualising the 5 classes. :::
